epics.title: User Authentication
epics.summary: Implement secure user authentication and authorization.
epics.description: Develop the user authentication system using React for the frontend implementation and FastAPI for the backend services. The system will handle user registrations, logins, password management, and OAuth integrations for social logins. Ensure that PostgreSQL is used for securely storing user credentials and profile information.

epics.title: Insurance Policy Data Model
epics.summary: Design and implement the insurance policy data model.
epics.description: Create the data model for insurance policies in PostgreSQL. Define schema for various policy types including health, motor, travel, and life insurance. Integrate with FastAPI backend to manage CRUD operations on the policy data.

epics.title: User Interface for Browsing Policies
epics.summary: Develop the UI for browsing and comparing insurance policies.
epics.description: Create a responsive user interface using React that allows users to browse, filter, and compare insurance policies. Implement components to display policy details, premiums, coverage, and benefits in an intuitive and user-friendly manner.

epics.title: Online Policy Purchase
epics.summary: Develop an online purchasing system for insurance policies.
epics.description: Implement a secure online purchasing system using React for the frontend and FastAPI for the backend. Integrate payment gateway APIs for handling transactions. Ensure that after successful purchase, digital policy documents are generated and sent to the user.

epics.title: User Account Management
epics.summary: Develop the user account management features for policy history, renewals, and claims.
epics.description: Create an account management module in React for displaying policy history, handling renewals, and tracking claims. Integrate with FastAPI backend to fetch and update account data stored in PostgreSQL. Implement secure profile management features.

epics.title: Administrator Dashboard
epics.summary: Implement dashboards for administrators and insurers to manage products, pricing, customers, policies, and claims.
epics.description: Develop comprehensive dashboards using React for administrators and insurance providers. Build backend services with FastAPI to support managing products, pricing, customer data, policies, and claims. Ensure data integrity and secure access to sensitive information stored in PostgreSQL.

epics.title: Agent Onboarding and Policy Approvals
epics.summary: Create features for agent onboarding and policy approval workflows.
epics.description: Implement workflows for onboarding insurance agents and handling policy approvals using React for the frontend and FastAPI for the backend services. Ensure secure handling of agent data and approval process information in PostgreSQL.

epics.title: Analytics and Reporting
epics.summary: Develop analytics and reporting features for administrators and insurers.
epics.description: Build the analytics and reporting module using React for the frontend visualization and FastAPI for backend data aggregation. Ensure efficient querying and reporting using PostgreSQL. Provide actionable insights and key metrics through interactive dashboards.

epics.title: Scalability and Performance Optimization
epics.summary: Ensure the platform is scalable and performs optimally under heavy load.
epics.description: Implement scalability and performance optimization techniques on both frontend (React) and backend (FastAPI). Optimize PostgreSQL database queries and indexing. Consider load balancing, caching strategies, and code optimizations to ensure smooth user experience.

epics.title: Mobile-Friendly Design
epics.summary: Ensure the platform is fully responsive and mobile-friendly.
epics.description: Adapt the entire user interface developed in React to be fully responsive and mobile-friendly. Ensure all features are accessible and performant on various mobile devices. Implement mobile-specific optimizations where necessary.

epics.title: Security Enhancements
epics.summary: Implement advanced security features for the digital insurance platform.
epics.description: Enhance the security of the platform by implementing features such as two-factor authentication, secure password policies, data encryption in the PostgreSQL database, and regular security audits. Ensure compliance with industry standards and best practices using FastAPI and React.

user_stories.title: Develop User Registration Functionality
user_stories.description:
As a new user, I want to register an account, so that I can access the application.

Core Domain Objects:
- User
- Credentials
- Profile

Attributes & Rules:
- New users must provide a valid email address.
- Password must be at least 8 characters long and include at least one number and one special character.
- User profiles should include a unique username.

Acceptance Criteria:

Scenario: Successful Registration
Given the registration form is accessed
When a new user provides valid registration details
Then the account is created successfully
And the user is redirected to the login page

Scenario: Invalid Email Format
Given the registration form is accessed
When a new user enters an invalid email address
Then an error message is displayed indicating the email format is invalid

Scenario: Weak Password
Given the registration form is accessed
When a new user enters a password that does not meet the complexity requirements
Then an error message is displayed indicating the password requirements

Scenario: Duplicate Username
Given the registration form is accessed
When a new user attempts to register with a username that is already taken
Then an error message is displayed indicating the username is already in use

Test Cases:

Given the registration form is accessed
When a new user provides valid registration details
Then the account is created successfully
And the user is redirected to the login page

Scenario: Weak Password

user_stories.title: Develop User Login Functionality

- Credentials
- Session

Attributes & Rules:
- Must ensure user credentials are verified against stored values.
- Lockout after 5 failed login attempts.
- Sessions should have a timeout period of 30 minutes of inactivity.

Acceptance Criteria:

Scenario: Successful Login
Given the login page is accessed
When the user provides valid credentials
Then the user is authenticated successfully
And the user is redirected to their dashboard

Scenario: Invalid Credentials
Given the login page is accessed
When the user provides invalid credentials
Then an error message is displayed indicating invalid username or password

Scenario: Account Lockout
Given the login page is accessed
When the user fails to log in after 5 attempts
Then the account is locked and a message is displayed indicating the account lockout
And the user is provided with instructions to reset the password

Scenario: Session Timeout
Given the user is logged in
When the user remains inactive for 30 minutes
Then the user's session is terminated
And the user is redirected to the login page with a session timeout message

user_stories.epic_title: Implement secure user authentication and authorization.
user_stories.title: Implement Password Management
user_stories.description:
As a registered user, I want to manage my account password, so that I can maintain the security of my account.

Core Domain Objects:
- User
- Credentials
- Password Reset Token

Attributes & Rules:
- Password reset token must expire after 24 hours.
- Password must meet complexity requirements during reset.
- Users must confirm their new password by entering it twice.

Acceptance Criteria:

Scenario: Successful Password Reset Request
Given the password reset request page is accessed
When the user enters a registered email address
Then a password reset link is sent to the user's email address
And a confirmation message is displayed indicating the email is sent

Scenario: Invalid Email Address
Given the password reset request page is accessed
When the user enters an unregistered email address
Then an error message is displayed indicating the email address is not found

Scenario: Successful Password Change
Given the user has received the password reset link
When the user accesses the link and provides a new password that meets the complexity requirements
And confirms the new password by entering it twice
Then the password is updated successfully
And the user can log in with the new password

Scenario: Expired Password Reset Token
Given the user has received the password reset link
When the user accesses the link after 24 hours
Then the token is marked as expired
And the user is prompted to request a new password reset

user_stories.title: Implement two-factor authentication
user_stories.description:
As a user, I want to be able to enable two-factor authentication, so that I can add an extra layer of security to my account.

Core Domain Objects:
- Authentication
- User
- Token

Attributes & Rules:
- Two-factor authentication should support both SMS and authenticator app methods.
- Users must be prompted to enable two-factor authentication after login.
- There must be an option to trust a device for 30 days, reducing the frequency of prompts.

Acceptance Criteria:

Scenario: User enables two-factor authentication via SMS
Given a user is logged in
When the user navigates to the security settings
And selects 'Enable two-factor authentication'
Then the user should choose SMS as the method
And receive an SMS with a verification code
And enter the code to confirm enabling two-factor authentication

Scenario: User enables two-factor authentication via authenticator app
Given a user is logged in
When the user navigates to the security settings
And selects 'Enable two-factor authentication'
Then the user should choose an authenticator app as the method
And scan the provided QR code with the app
And enter the code generated by the app to confirm enabling two-factor authentication

Scenario: User skips enabling two-factor authentication
Given a user is logged in
When the user is prompted to enable two-factor authentication
Then the user chooses to skip the prompt
And the user is reminded at the next login

user_stories.title: Implement secure password policies
user_stories.description:
As a user, I want to follow secure password policies, so that my account credentials are protected.

Core Domain Objects:
- User
- Password

Attributes & Rules:
- Passwords must be at least 12 characters long.
- Passwords must contain upper and lower case letters, numbers, and special characters.
- Passwords should not contain commonly used passwords or patterns (e.g., 'password123').

Acceptance Criteria:

Scenario: User sets a password meeting the requirements
Given a user is setting a password
When the user enters a compliant password
Then the password is accepted
And the user can proceed to the next step

Scenario: User attempts to set a weak password
Given a user is setting a password
When the user enters a weak password
Then the system should reject the password
And provide feedback on the requirements

Scenario: User resets password to one previously used
Given a user is resetting the password
When the user attempts to reuse an old password
Then the system should reject the password
And prompt the user to choose a different one

user_stories.title: Implement data encryption in PostgreSQL database
user_stories.description:
As a database administrator, I want to ensure that all sensitive data is encrypted in the PostgreSQL database, so that it is protected from unauthorized access.

Core Domain Objects:
- Data
- Encryption Key

Attributes & Rules:
- Data encryption should be enabled at rest and in transit.
- Encryption keys must be rotated regularly.
- Sensitive data fields must be encrypted using AES-256 encryption standard.

Acceptance Criteria:

Scenario: Encrypt sensitive data at rest
Given sensitive data in the database
When data is stored
Then it must be encrypted with AES-256
And accessible only with the appropriate encryption key

Scenario: Encrypt data in transit
Given data is transmitted between server and client
When transmission occurs
Then data must be encrypted and transmitted over HTTPS

Scenario: Rotate encryption keys
Given an existing encryption system
When the encryption keys are due for rotation
Then generate new keys
And re-encrypt existing data with the new keys

user_stories.title: Conduct regular security audits
user_stories.description:
As a security officer, I want to conduct regular security audits, so that I can ensure compliance with industry standards and identify vulnerabilities.

Core Domain Objects:
- Security Audit
- Compliance Report

Attributes & Rules:
- Security audits must be conducted quarterly.
- Compliance with industry standards must be documented.
- All identified vulnerabilities should be addressed within 30 days.

Acceptance Criteria:

Scenario: Schedule regular security audits
Given the need for periodic security checks
When planning the security audits
Then schedule the audits to be conducted every quarter

Scenario: Document compliance with industry standards
Given a security audit
When an audit is conducted
Then document compliance with relevant industry standards
And generate a compliance report

Scenario: Address identified vulnerabilities
Given vulnerabilities identified by an audit
When the audit is complete
Then create tickets to address each vulnerability
And ensure they are resolved within 30 days
